heading: Authority to denature
label: 5241 Authority to denature
text: Under such regulations as the Secretary shall prescribe, distilled spirits may be denatured on the bonded premises of a distilled spirits plant qualified for the processing of distilled spirits. Distilled spirits to be denatured under this section shall be of such kind and such degree of proof as the Secretary shall by regulations prescribe. Distilled spirits denatured under this section may be used on the bonded premises of a distilled spirits plant in the manufacture of any article.
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 5241, , , related to supervision of operations of internal revenue bonded warehouses, prior to the general revision of this chapter by . See sections 5201(a), 5202 (a), (c), (d), and 7803 of this title and section 22 of former Title 5, Executive Departments and Government Officers and Employees.act Aug. 16, 1954, ch. 73668A Stat. 644Pub. L. 85–859

Amendments1979— substituted “a distilled spirits plant qualified for the processing of distilled spirits” for “any distilled spirits plant operated by a proprietor who is authorized to produce distilled spirits at such plant or on other bonded premises”, struck out provision that any other person operating bonded premises may, at the discretion of the Secretary and under such regulations as he may prescribe, be authorized to denature distilled spirits on such bonded premises, and inserted provision that distilled spirits denatured under this section may be used on the bonded premises of a distilled spirits plant in the manufacture of any article.Pub. L. 96–391976— struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 94–455
